comment_text.sn3.15:1.8: The background is explained in Ja 239. Pasenadi’s father, Mahākosala, had forged an alliance by marrying his daughter to Bimbisāra, endowing her with the revenues of a Kāsi village. Ajātasattu gained the throne by murdering his father, and the queen—Pasenadi’s sister Kosaladevī—died of grief soon after. So when he claimed the village, Pasenadi was outraged at the thought of his father’s gift going to such a man. He took the village, and the series of back-and-forth battles began.
comment_text.sn3.15:7.4: See Bodhi’s Connected Discourses, note 263 for this line.
comment_text.sn3.15:8.1: The idiom here is tricky. Bodhi has, “The fool thinks fortune is on his side”. The commentary glosses ṭhāna with kāraṇa (“reason”). I think ṭhāna (“solid ground”) here sets up the “fall” at the end of the verse.
comment_text.sn3.15:9.1: The Buddha speaks of the tendency for evil conduct to be passed down through generations, a fate that was to befall Ajātasattu himself.

root_text.sn3.15:1.1: Atha kho rājā māgadho ajātasattu vedehiputto caturaṅginiṁ senaṁ sannayhitvā rājānaṁ pasenadiṁ kosalaṁ abbhuyyāsi yena kāsi.
root_text.sn3.15:1.3: “rājā kira māgadho ajātasattu vedehiputto caturaṅginiṁ senaṁ sannayhitvā mamaṁ abbhuyyāto yena kāsī”ti.
root_text.sn3.15:1.4: Atha kho rājā pasenadi kosalo caturaṅginiṁ senaṁ sannayhitvā rājānaṁ māgadhaṁ ajātasattuṁ vedehiputtaṁ paccuyyāsi yena kāsi.
root_text.sn3.15:1.5: Atha kho rājā ca māgadho ajātasattu vedehiputto rājā ca pasenadi kosalo saṅgāmesuṁ.
root_text.sn3.15:1.6: Tasmiṁ kho pana saṅgāme rājā pasenadi kosalo rājānaṁ māgadhaṁ ajātasattuṁ vedehiputtaṁ parājesi, jīvaggāhañca naṁ aggahesi.
root_text.sn3.15:1.7: Atha kho rañño pasenadissa kosalassa etadahosi:
root_text.sn3.15:1.8: “kiñcāpi kho myāyaṁ rājā māgadho ajātasattu vedehiputto adubbhantassa dubbhati, atha ca pana me bhāgineyyo hoti.
root_text.sn3.15:1.9: Yannūnāhaṁ rañño māgadhassa ajātasattuno vedehiputtassa sabbaṁ hatthikāyaṁ pariyādiyitvā sabbaṁ assakāyaṁ pariyādiyitvā sabbaṁ rathakāyaṁ pariyādiyitvā sabbaṁ pattikāyaṁ pariyādiyitvā jīvantameva naṁ osajjeyyan”ti.
root_text.sn3.15:2.1: Atha kho rājā pasenadi kosalo rañño māgadhassa ajātasattuno vedehiputtassa sabbaṁ hatthikāyaṁ pariyādiyitvā sabbaṁ assakāyaṁ pariyādiyitvā sabbaṁ rathakāyaṁ pariyādiyitvā sabbaṁ pattikāyaṁ pariyādiyitvā jīvantameva naṁ osajji.
root_text.sn3.15:3.1: Atha kho sambahulā bhikkhū pubbaṇhasamayaṁ nivāsetvā pattacīvaramādāya sāvatthiṁ piṇḍāya pavisiṁsu.
root_text.sn3.15:3.2: Sāvatthiyaṁ piṇḍāya caritvā pacchābhattaṁ piṇḍapātapaṭikkantā yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkamiṁsu; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdiṁsu. Ekamantaṁ nisinnā kho te bhikkhū bhagavantaṁ etadavocuṁ:
root_text.sn3.15:4.1: “Idha, bhante, rājā māgadho ajātasattu vedehiputto caturaṅginiṁ senaṁ sannayhitvā rājānaṁ pasenadiṁ kosalaṁ abbhuyyāsi yena kāsi.
root_text.sn3.15:4.2: Assosi kho, bhante, rājā pasenadi kosalo:
root_text.sn3.15:4.3: ‘rājā kira māgadho ajātasattu vedehiputto caturaṅginiṁ senaṁ sannayhitvā mamaṁ abbhuyyāto yena kāsī’ti.
root_text.sn3.15:4.4: Atha kho, bhante, rājā pasenadi kosalo caturaṅginiṁ senaṁ sannayhitvā rājānaṁ māgadhaṁ ajātasattuṁ vedehiputtaṁ paccuyyāsi yena kāsi.
root_text.sn3.15:4.5: Atha kho, bhante, rājā ca māgadho ajātasattu vedehiputto rājā ca pasenadi kosalo saṅgāmesuṁ.
root_text.sn3.15:4.6: Tasmiṁ kho pana, bhante, saṅgāme rājā pasenadi kosalo rājānaṁ māgadhaṁ ajātasattuṁ vedehiputtaṁ parājesi, jīvaggāhañca naṁ aggahesi.
root_text.sn3.15:4.7: Atha kho, bhante, rañño pasenadissa kosalassa etadahosi:
root_text.sn3.15:4.8: ‘kiñcāpi kho myāyaṁ rājā māgadho ajātasattu vedehiputto adubbhantassa dubbhati, atha ca pana me bhāgineyyo hoti.
root_text.sn3.15:4.9: Yannūnāhaṁ rañño māgadhassa ajātasattuno vedehiputtassa sabbaṁ hatthikāyaṁ pariyādiyitvā sabbaṁ assakāyaṁ sabbaṁ rathakāyaṁ sabbaṁ pattikāyaṁ pariyādiyitvā jīvantameva naṁ osajjeyyan’”ti.
root_text.sn3.15:5.1: “Atha kho, bhante, rājā pasenadi kosalo rañño māgadhassa ajātasattuno vedehiputtassa sabbaṁ hatthikāyaṁ pariyādiyitvā sabbaṁ assakāyaṁ pariyādiyitvā sabbaṁ rathakāyaṁ pariyādiyitvā sabbaṁ pattikāyaṁ pariyādiyitvā jīvantameva naṁ osajjī”ti.
root_text.sn3.15:6.1: Atha kho bhagavā etamatthaṁ viditvā tāyaṁ velāyaṁ imā gāthāyo abhāsi:

translation_text.sn3.15:1.1: Then King Ajātasattu of Magadha, son of the princess of Videha, mobilized an army of four divisions and marched to Kāsi to attack King Pasenadi of Kosala.
translation_text.sn3.15:1.2: When King Pasenadi heard of this,
translation_text.sn3.15:1.4: he mobilized an army of four divisions and marched to Kāsi to defend it against Ajātasattu.
translation_text.sn3.15:1.5: Then the two kings met in battle.
translation_text.sn3.15:1.6: And in that battle Pasenadi defeated Ajātasattu and captured him alive.
translation_text.sn3.15:1.8: “Even though I’ve never betrayed this King Ajātasattu, he betrayed me. Still, he is my nephew.
translation_text.sn3.15:1.9: Now that I’ve vanquished all of Ajātasattu’s elephant troops, cavalry, chariots, and infantry, why don’t I let him loose with just his life?”
translation_text.sn3.15:3.2: told the Buddha what had happened.
translation_text.sn3.15:6.1: Then, understanding this matter, on that occasion the Buddha recited these verses:
translation_text.sn3.15:7.2: as long as it serves his ends.
translation_text.sn3.15:7.3: But as soon as others plunder him,
translation_text.sn3.15:8.1: For the fool thinks they’re on solid ground,
translation_text.sn3.15:8.2: so long as their wickedness has not ripened.
translation_text.sn3.15:8.3: But as soon as that wickedness ripens,
translation_text.sn3.15:9.4: and a bully creates a bully.

variant_text.sn3.15:1.9: osajjeyyan”ti → ossajjeyyanti (bj, sya-all, km, pts1ed, pts2ed)
variant_text.sn3.15:2.1: osajji → ossajji (bj, sya-all, km, pts1ed, pts2ed)
variant_text.sn3.15:7.4: viluppati → vilumpati (bj, sya-all, pts1ed, pts2ed, mr)

reference_text.sn3.15:4.1: bj13.158, cck15.117, ms12S1_666, ndp12.84, pts-vp-pli1ed1.85, pts-vp-pli2ed1.192, sya15.124
